title: OpenClaw multi-account Discord setups should not lose slash-command and shortcut behavior

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #54915.

task.summary: Diagnose why Discord slash commands and inline shortcuts degrade in a multi-account setup.
task.instructions: Use the evidence from the multi-account setup to confirm the broken slash and shortcut behavior, localize it to the Discord command parsing/dispatch path, and propose an ops-level repair plan that restores the expected responses.
task.acceptance_notes: This GitHub-derived ops scenario is synthetic and graded behaviorally.

visible_context.issue_text: In a multi-account Discord setup, slash commands return Done while inline shortcuts stop behaving like commands.
error_logs: slash command /status returns Done with no content
inline shortcut text reaches the model instead of being handled as a command
reproduction_steps: Explain where command parsing and dispatch diverge from the expected path.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models command-path diagnosis in multi-account Discord deployments.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic Discord multi-account command incident derived from the GitHub issue.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #54915.
